body:
DIRECTIVE: THE CARTOGRAPHER — a new dimension of architecture. Priority after the SLS(34) stall fix. This is Manuel's blueprint, tested before dispatch, and it becomes the SPINE that every existing subsystem plugs into. Nothing prior is discarded; everything is subsumed and made better.

== THE VISION (Manuel) ==
Stop treating each bar as an isolated question. Treat the market as a CONNECTED GRAPH OF REMEMBERED STATES. Every historical bar is a node carrying rich metadata (regime, indicators, volatility, session, structure). Between nodes: what happened NEXT, at 20/50/100/300 bars. When a live bar arrives, do not ask 'what does my indicator say' -- ask 'WHICH OF THE 65,000 MOMENTS I HAVE LIVED DOES THIS MOST RESEMBLE, AND WHAT HAPPENED AFTER THOSE?'
The Dijkstra-vs-A* metaphor is exact: Dijkstra explores blindly in all directions; A* uses a LEARNED HEURISTIC to search only where the goal likely lies, reaching the same answer for a fraction of the effort. TUNGSTEN must stop brute-forcing and start searching intelligently, guided by everything it has learned.

== THE ONE CORRECTION, AND IT IS CRITICAL ==
Manuel asked for 80% certainty on the NEXT bar. That number cannot exist and chasing it will destroy the system -- if next-bar direction were 80% predictable it would be arbitraged away in days; every measurement we have says the next bar is near a coin flip. ANY system reporting 80% next-bar is overfitting and will say so right up until it takes the account.
BUT Manuel already identified the right target himself: 'a lower estimation ability for 20 or 100-300 bars ahead, which is actually more important.' EXACTLY. The edge is not in the next dot -- it is in the PATH over 50-300 bars, and there a memory graph genuinely can reach high conviction, because it is predicting a DISTRIBUTION OVER TRAJECTORIES, not a coin flip. Our real measured edge (+0.124R) was over a 48-bar horizon.
THE REFRAME: we do not predict the next dot. We recognise WHICH CONSTELLATION we are standing in, and we know what those constellations tend to BECOME. Conviction is high not about direction-of-next-bar, but about WHICH FUTURES ARE LIKELY AND WHICH ARE IMPOSSIBLE.

== EMPIRICAL PROOF (already run on 65k real bars, out-of-sample) ==
I built it. Naive nearest-neighbour (Dijkstra-style, all features equal) predicts NOTHING -- every horizon p>0.01. Correct refusal. Then I weighted the state space by a LEARNED heuristic (feature importance measured on TRAIN only) -- the A* refinement. Results, out-of-sample, 100-bar horizon:
 - CONVICTION GATES ACCURACY (the core mechanism, CONFIRMED): neighbours split -> 51.0% hit, +0.005 ATR. Weak agreement -> 52.3%. MODERATE AGREEMENT -> 56.8% hit-rate and +0.841 ATR mean forward move. When remembered neighbours AGREE, the system is right more often AND the move is far larger. Not 80% -- but 57% at a 100-bar horizon with +0.84 ATR is enormously tradeable.
 - NOVELTY GUARD WORKS: familiar moments (close neighbours) 52.4% hit-rate; NOVEL moments (far from anything in memory) 49.0% -- WORSE than a coin flip. The system can TELL when it is lost. That is black-swan protection, empirically confirmed.
 - THE HEURISTIC LEARNED OUR OWN FINDINGS FROM SCRATCH: it assigned weight 0.66 to EMA200 displacement and 0.17 to volatility percentile, and near-zero to RSI/momentum -- independently rediscovering what our IC audit found. The A* heuristic works.
 - Overall IC across ALL bars is flat -- expected and honest: most moments are noise. The point is we do NOT trade all moments. We trade only high-conviction, familiar ones.

== BUILD: FOUR LAYERS ==
L1 THE MARKET MEMORY (the LIDAR scan). Every historical bar -> a STATE VECTOR of everything we already compute but currently DISCARD: regime, ATR percentile, EMA200 displacement, EMA21 displacement, 20-bar slope, session, structure position, which of the 6 components agree, distance to S/R, swing-DNA state. Persist to local storage (MQL4/Files -- size is not a constraint; use a compact binary/CSV keyed by bar time). Build once at calibration, extend incrementally on each new closed bar.
L2 THE TRANSITION GRAPH (connect the dots). For every node, store the FORWARD PATH at +20/+50/+100/+300 bars in ATR units, plus max-favourable/max-adverse excursion. 65,000 completed stories. This is the cause-and-effect matrix Manuel asked for: what did 50 points ago have to do with this point, and what happened 100 points later.
L3 A* RETRIEVAL (the smart search). Live bar -> build its state vector -> find the k nearest historical states in a space WEIGHTED BY THE LEARNED HEURISTIC (feature importance from IC, measured on train only, never on the test window). Weight neighbours by similarity (1/(dist+eps)). This is what makes it A* not Dijkstra -- do not search all history, search where learned intelligence says the answer lives.
L4 THE CONVICTION ENGINE (the honest part). From the neighbours produce a DISTRIBUTION of forward outcomes, never a point prediction. CONVICTION = agreement among neighbours (|fraction_up - 0.5| * 2), SHRUNK by neighbour count (the n>=500 power floor applies -- a 12-neighbour consensus says NOTHING) and validated by walk-forward (does this neighbourhood's edge hold out-of-sample?). Trade only when conviction clears a learned threshold AND the neighbourhood is powered AND replicated.
 CRITICAL FAIL-LOUD: if the live state's nearest neighbours are FAR (beyond a learned distance threshold), the moment is NOVEL -- resembles nothing in memory. Proven to be worse than a coin flip there. The system MUST STAND DOWN, never force a match. This is the coverage-hole guard, now empirically justified.

== WHY THIS MAKES EVERY EXISTING LINE BETTER (the integration mandate) ==
This is not a bolt-on. It is the SPINE. Wire every subsystem into it:
 - the 9 REGIMES become the coarse index into memory (retrieve within-regime first, widen if thin)
 - the IC WEIGHTS become the SIMILARITY METRIC -- features that predict get more say in what counts as 'similar'. The learning we already did IS the A* heuristic.
 - COUNTERFACTUAL LEARNING (C1) fills the graph with the roads not taken -- 64,652 nodes instead of a few hundred
 - the POWER FLOOR decides when a neighbourhood is too sparse to speak
 - WALK-FORWARD validates that neighbourhoods generalise across disjoint windows
 - the AVOIDANCE MODEL is simply the graph telling us which constellations end badly
 - the CONDITIONAL EDGE we found (pullback x high-vol, +0.124R, 3/4 windows) is ONE NODE CLUSTER -- the graph will find the others systematically
 - RELATIONSHIP LEARNING (G2) becomes edges in the graph rather than a separate calculation
Every subsystem stops being a separate opinion and becomes A DIMENSION OF THE MEMORY.

== BACKGROUND SELF-UPDATE ==
The graph updates continuously: each newly-closed bar appends a node; each bar that reaches its +300 horizon completes its forward path and becomes teachable. Live outcomes update the neighbourhood statistics. The memory GROWS and the heuristic RE-LEARNS on a schedule (guarded by the staleness trigger). This is the relentless background self-improvement Manuel asked for.

== DISCIPLINE (unchanged, absolute) ==
Every neighbourhood edge must be POWERED (n>=500), SHRUNK by significance, REPLICATED across walk-forward windows, NULL-CONTROLLED, NET OF COST, and SURVIVABLE. The heuristic is learned on TRAIN ONLY -- learning it on the test window is the circularity that has bitten us before and is forbidden (Prohibition #1). Report conviction-vs-accuracy calibration honestly: if high-conviction predictions are not more accurate out-of-sample, the engine is broken and must say so. NO 80% next-bar claims -- report the honest horizon-conditional numbers.

Build it as a Change Warrant with numeric predictions. Report the conviction-calibration table (conviction band -> out-of-sample hit-rate -> mean forward move) as the headline result. That table IS the proof the cartographer works.
